milestone: My Cloud Worlds — Classification Redesign: Linked / Related / Orphan
slice: A real screenshot showed "My World Projects" and "My Cloud Worlds" appearing to list duplicate copies of the same World; the user escalated from "explain this" to "figure out how to easily interpret them, showing multiple copies of same theme is confusing -- there should be a better way to present it, also the operations available." Root cause: a Cloud World card matched a local Project only by exact storage id, never by World identity, so a Cloud backup left behind by an earlier Duplicate/rename rendered as an apparently unrelated card. Redesigned into three classified cases, reusing the existing World-identity-matching technique from _annotateProjectBadges rather than a new heuristic: Linked (row.id matches a local Project exactly) is folded into that local card's own inline status line instead of a second card; Related (no Project-id match, but the same World identity as a local Project) gets its own explicitly-labelled card with an honest "Open as a Separate Copy" action reusing the exact _duplicateProject() mechanism; Orphan (no match at all) is the genuine new-device sync case, enriched with real Last Saved + scene count. Verified via a new 23-assertion Playwright suite covering all three cases end-to-end plus full regression across the existing sign-in/versioned-sync/thumbnail/delete suites (two of which had their own assertions updated, not weakened, since they directly tested the old, now-superseded flat-list behavior for the Linked case) and both goldenBuild.js suites, all unchanged. Cache-bust ?v= bumped 0098->0099 since this touches worldBuilderApp.js, world-builder.css, and index.html together. Confined to js/worldBuilderApp.js, css/world-builder.css, and index.html.
